headline: Oracle Red Bull Racing Alliance Puts AI Spotlight On Oracle Valuation
summary: Oracle and Oracle Red Bull Racing have agreed to a multi year expansion of their partnership focused on AI and cloud driven race strategy and engineering. Oracle AI and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure will serve as the digital backbone for Red Bull Racing as Formula 1 prepares for major regulatory changes in 2026. The collaboration centers on real time decision making, hybrid powertrain development and advanced simulations for the team. For investors watching NYSE:ORCL, this announcement...

headline: Big Tech signs Trump's AI energy cost pledge
summary: President Trump announced a new "ratepayer protection pledge," saying energy demand tied to AI data centers could more than triple by 2035, promising American households won't see higher electricity bills as a result. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Jennifer Schonberger joins Yahoo Finance host Josh Lipton to discuss the big tech companies under the agreement, Google (GOOG), Microsoft (MSFT), Oracle (ORCL), OpenAI (OPAI.PVT), Amazon (AMZN), and xAI, and their pledge to fully fund new power generation and infrastructure needed for their AI facilities. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination.

headline: Tech Companies Pledge to Pay More for Power
summary: Seven big tech companies that build data centers to train artificial-intelligence models promised to pay more for electricity and limit power price increases for consumers, a pledge President Trump has touted as key to addressing a backlash to AI.

headline: HubSpot and Other Software Stocks Are Bouncing Back. Their Next Move Could Come Soon.
summary: The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector Exchange-Traded fund—which owns large software names such as  Microsoft Oracle Salesforce  Adobe, and  Palantir  —is up 12% since late February, after a brutal monthslong selloff.  The market is concerned that the privately-owned AI pioneers Anthropic and OpenAI will take market share from the many software vendors that provide tools to businesses and people.  The industry’s early recovery comes as bargain hunters take a chance on these beaten-down names, betting that some can still thrive despite AI.

headline: Soaring Power Prices Have US Politicians Searching for Solutions
summary: The Energy Department has loaned $26.5 billion — the most in its history — to help a big Southeastern utility reduce the cost of building new power plants in Georgia and Alabama.  Meanwhile, the nation’s biggest grid operator has proposed capping wholesale prices and some governors are weighing utility rate freezes as a way to keep a lid on electricity costs.  The affordability problem is prompting a White House meeting Wednesday with technology executives.

The affordability problem is prompting a White House meeting Wednesday with technology executives.
